instructions.detail: Put all ingredients (1 lb ground pork, 1 egg, ¼ cup breadcrumbs, all seasonings) in a bowl. Mix thoroughly.
Form into 8-9 balls and flatten into a patty shape, approximately 10-15% larger than your bread.
Put a large pan on medium-high heat.
Add the patties and cook to an internal temp of 160°F, flipping halfway. About 7 minutes per side.
Crack 2 eggs into a bowl, add 1 Tbsp milk and 1 tsp Tony's seasoning. Whisk until combined.
Pour into a non-stick pan over medium-low heat. Gently fold the eggs with a spatula until just set.
Add milk (7 cups), oats (2 cups), honey/syrup (2 Tbsp), and salt (2 tsp) to a slow cooker.
Set on low for 8 hours. Stir every 2 hours if possible.
When 2 hours remain, add frozen fruit (3 cups) after stirring.
Serve with jelly or fresh fruit for added sweetness.

ingredients.text: 2-3 lb chuck roast, top round, or London broil

instructions.detail: Coat meat in salt and pepper.
Sear on all sides for 2-3 minutes each side.
Cook on low in slow cooker for 6-8 hours.
Rinse and dry pork shoulder (4 lb). Rub all over with salt (2½ tsp) and pepper (1 tsp).
Combine Meat Rub ingredients (1 Tbsp dried oregano, 2 tsp cumin, 1 Tbsp olive oil), then rub all over the pork.
Place pork fat-cap-up in slow cooker. Top with chopped onion, jalapeño, minced garlic (4 cloves), and squeeze over orange juice (¾ cup).
Cook on low for 10 hours or high for 7 hours.
Remove pork and let cool slightly. Shred with two forks. Skim fat from remaining juices. Reduce juices to about 2 cups if needed.

Heat 1 Tbsp oil in a large skillet over high heat. Spread pork and drizzle with juices. Wait for juices to evaporate and bottom to brown and crisp. Turn briefly to sear other side. Remove and repeat in batches.
Drizzle with remaining juices just before serving. Stuff in tacos or serve over rice.

ingredients.text: ⅓ cup (80ml) whole milk, warmed to ~110°F
2¼ tsp (7g) active dry or instant yeast (1 standard packet)
1 tsp fresh lemon juice (or apple cider vinegar)
1⅓ cups (173g) whole wheat flour (plus more as needed)

instructions.detail: Whisk warm water (1 cup), warm milk (⅓ cup), and yeast (2¼ tsp) in a large bowl. Add 2 cups flour and stir until a thick batter forms. Cover and let double in size at room temperature, about 60-90 minutes.

Add honey (3 Tbsp), butter (3 Tbsp), lemon juice (1 tsp), remaining flour (1⅓ cups), and salt (1¼ tsp). Using a dough hook or by hand, mix on medium until dough pulls away from sides, about 3 minutes.
Knead in mixer or by hand on a lightly floured surface for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Lightly grease a large bowl. Place dough in bowl, turning to coat. Cover and let rise in a warm spot for 1.5-2 hours until doubled.
Grease a 9×5-inch loaf pan.
Punch down dough to release air. On a lightly floured surface, roll into an 8×15-inch rectangle. Roll up into an 8-inch log and place in loaf pan.
Cover shaped loaf and let rise until about 1 inch above the top of the pan, about 45-60 minutes.
Adjust oven rack to a lower position and preheat to 350°F (177°C).
Optional: Brush risen loaf with beaten egg white, then sprinkle oats (1 Tbsp) on top.
Bake for 36-40 minutes until golden brown. Bread is done when it sounds hollow when tapped, or an instant-read thermometer reads 195-200°F (90-93°C).
Remove from oven and cool for a few minutes in pan on a cooling rack before slicing.

name: Garlic Butter Chicken and Potatoes Skillet

ingredients.text: 1½ lb (650g) chicken breast, cut into strips
1 tsp each fresh thyme, rosemary, and oregano, chopped
¼ cup low-sodium soy sauce (or coconut aminos)

instructions.detail: Combine chicken (1½ lb strips) with soy sauce (¼ cup), olive oil (2 Tbsp), hot sauce (2 Tbsp), and a pinch of pepper. Toss well and set aside while you prepare potatoes.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add quartered potatoes (1½ lb) and boil for 8 minutes. Drain.

Heat 1 Tbsp olive oil and 1 Tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add drained potatoes and sauté 4 minutes, then stir and cook another 4-5 minutes until golden and fork-tender. Transfer to a plate.

Using same skillet, reduce heat to medium. Add remaining 2 Tbsp butter. Add marinated chicken (drain and reserve marinade), minced garlic (5 cloves), and optional chili flakes. Cook 1 minute per side until lightly browned.
Pour reserved marinade into skillet and simmer for a few minutes until thickened and chicken is cooked through. Stir in 1 tsp each sage, onion powder, and garlic powder.
Return potatoes to skillet. Toss to combine and heat through 1-2 minutes. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Remove from heat, add cheese and spinach (1 cup), and serve.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Combine ground beef (2 lb), ground pork (1 lb), onion soup mix (2 packets), eggs (2), panko (1 cup), and Worcestershire sauce (2 Tbsp). Mix until just combined.
Mix ketchup (½ cup), brown sugar (¼ cup), lemon juice (1 Tbsp), and mustard (2 tsp) in a small bowl.
Shape meat mixture into a loaf in a baking pan. Spread half the glaze on top.
Bake for 1 hour. Apply remaining glaze during the last 15 minutes. Internal temperature should reach 160°F.
Let rest 10 minutes before slicing.
Combine all marinade ingredients. Add chicken drumsticks (2 lb) and marinate for 4-8 hours.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Set a wire rack over a baking pan.
Place drumsticks on wire rack. Cook for 30-35 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Broil for 3-5 minutes until skin is crispy.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add ground beef (1 lb) and cook, breaking up the meat, until mostly browned. Drain grease and return to heat.
Add diced onion (½ cup), green bell pepper (1), and potatoes (2 cups). Continue cooking over medium heat until onion and peppers are softened. Add garlic (4 cloves) and cook an additional 30 seconds.
Pour in tomato sauce (8-16 oz) and beef broth (½ cup). Add salt (1 tsp), pepper (¼ tsp), cumin (1 tsp), coriander (1 tsp), and bay leaf (1). Stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove lid, stir, and continue cooking uncovered for 10 minutes until potatoes are soft and most liquid is gone.
Serve warm with flour tortillas and rice.

ingredients.text: 4 oz Velveeta, cut into cubes (can sub ¾ cup shredded cheese)
1 can (10 oz) Rotel tomatoes with green chilies, undrained

instructions.detail: Shred cheddar and Monterey Jack from a block. Let cheese come to near room temperature before adding to sauce.
Cook and crumble ground beef (1 lb) in a high-walled pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Drain grease.
Melt butter (1 Tbsp) in the same pot. Add garlic (2 cloves) and cook for 1 minute.
Add taco seasoning (1 packet), Worcestershire sauce (1 Tbsp), tomato paste (2 Tbsp), beef broth (1 cup), chicken broth (1 cup), milk (1 cup), and Rotel tomatoes (1 can). Stir to combine.
Bring to a gentle boil. Add pasta shells (½ lb) and submerge. Cover and cook according to package instructions, stirring halfway through.
Remove cover and ensure pasta is cooked. Taste-test for doneness. Cover and cook longer if needed.
Turn heat to low. Gradually stir in all three cheeses until melted.
Sauce will thicken as it stands. Serve once desired consistency is reached.
Rinse and dry pork shoulder (4 lb). Mix all Rub ingredients together, then rub all over the pork.
Place pork fat-cap-up in slow cooker. Top with chopped onion, jalapeño, minced garlic (4 cloves), and squeeze over orange juice (¾ cup).
Cook on low for 10 hours or high for 7 hours until pork is tender enough to shred.
Remove pork and let cool slightly. Shred with two forks. Skim fat from remaining juices. If more than 2 cups of liquid remain, reduce down.

Heat 1 Tbsp oil in a large skillet over high heat. Spread pork in pan and drizzle with juices. Wait until juices evaporate and bottom side is golden and crusty. Turn and briefly sear other side. Work in batches - do not crowd the pan.
Drizzle with remaining juices just before serving. Serve hot in tacos.
Combine all ingredients in a large bowl.
Refrigerate for 1+ hours before serving.

ingredients.text: 1/4 cup green onion whites, cut into 1-inch pieces

instructions.detail: Soak the broccoli (16 oz) in clean water 10-15 min, dunking and rinsing. Cut florets from stem, slicing up from the stem with a paring knife to keep buds intact. Peel stem skin and slice thin. Return everything to clean water, rinse again, drain.

Slice chicken breast (12 oz) thin. Combine cornstarch (1 Tbsp), water (2 Tbsp), salt (1/2 tsp), oyster sauce (1 Tbsp), and white pepper (1/4 tsp); mix until cornstarch dissolves, then squeeze the marinade into the chicken with your hand. Set aside.

Smash, peel, and mince the garlic (2 cloves). Peel and cut ginger (1 Tbsp) into small triangles. Slice green onion whites (1/4 cup) into 1-inch pieces. Peel carrot (1/4 cup) and cut thin on a diagonal.

Sauce: combine oyster sauce (1 Tbsp), light soy sauce (1 Tbsp), salt (1/2 tsp), sugar (1 1/2 tsp), cooking wine (2 tsp), white pepper (1/3 tsp). Set aside. Slurry: stir cornstarch (1 Tbsp) into water (2 Tbsp). Keep separate until the last step.

Bring water (8 cups) to a rolling boil. Add salt (1 tsp) and oil (2 tsp) — keeps the broccoli bright green. Add broccoli; the moment water returns to a boil, pull it out (4-5 min max). Shock in ice water 1 min, drain, set aside.

Heat wok on high until smoking. Add oil (2 Tbsp), drop heat to low. Lay chicken in a single layer; let it brown undisturbed ~30 sec before flipping. Stir fry until just cooked through, ~2 min total. Remove and set aside.

Add garlic (2 cloves), ginger (1 Tbsp), green onion whites (1/4 cup), and carrot (1/4 cup) to the hot wok; stir fry 30-40 sec until fragrant. Crank heat to high. Add broccoli and cooking wine (1 tsp); stir fry 1 1/2-2 min. Return chicken; stir fry 30 sec. Pour sauce into the wok center; toss 15-20 sec. Taste — add sugar (1/2 tsp) if needed. Drizzle in slurry while stirring, ~30 sec, until glossy and lightly coated. Finish with sesame oil (1 tsp), toss once, kill the heat. Plate immediately.

ingredients.text: ¼ cup water or chicken broth

instructions.detail: Toss chicken in marinade ingredients. Cook through in a pan with oil, then slice or shred.
Whisk all sauce ingredients together. Set aside.
Cook brown rice noodles per package directions. Drain and rinse under cold water to prevent overcooking. Reserve ¼ cup noodle water.
Add carrots (2, julienned) and oil (1 Tbsp) to a hot wok or large skillet. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add garlic (3-5 cloves, minced) and green onions in the last 1-2 minutes.
Add noodles, cooked chicken, and sauce. Add a splash of reserved noodle water as needed to loosen.
Stir, heat through, and serve immediately.

ingredients.text: 1 lb chicken breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions.detail: Combine garlic (3 cloves, minced), salt (1 tsp), brown sugar (2 tsp), water (2 Tbsp), rice wine (1 Tbsp), baking soda (¼ tsp), and soy sauce (2 Tbsp). Stir to dissolve sugar.
Add chicken pieces (1 lb) to marinade and toss to coat. Marinate for 30 minutes.
Beat egg in one bowl. Place cornstarch (¾ cup) in another bowl.
Drain chicken from marinade. Dip each piece in egg wash, then coat thoroughly in cornstarch.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry chicken in batches (do not overcrowd), about 4-5 minutes per batch until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Return fried chicken to pan. Toss with salt (½ tsp) and pepper (½ tsp) to taste. Garnish with chopped green onions (2 Tbsp) and serve hot.

ingredients.text: ½ red bell pepper, diced (~¾ cup)
1½ cups pineapple pieces, fresh or canned drained

instructions.detail: Heat vegetable oil (2 Tbsp) in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Add garlic (2 cloves) and onion (½). Cook for 1 minute.
Add red bell pepper (½) and cook for 1 minute.
Add frozen peas (½ cup) and stir for 30 seconds.
Add day-old rice (3 cups) and all sauce ingredients (oyster sauce, fish sauce, sugar, soy sauce, Thai seasoning). Cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes until rice is caramelized.
Add pineapple pieces (1½ cups) and stir for 30 seconds to warm through.
Stir through green onions (½ cup) and serve immediately.

ingredients.text: ¼ cup extra virgin olive oil

instructions.detail: Whisk together olive oil (¼ cup), rice vinegar (¼ cup), honey (1½ Tbsp), sesame oil (3 Tbsp), soy sauce (1½ tsp), salt, and garlic.
Toss spinach (3 cups) with cranberries (½ cup) and walnuts (½ cup). Drizzle with dressing and serve.
Slice pork into 1-inch strips.
Use a fork to poke many holes all over each piece to help the marinade absorb.
In a bowl, combine garlic salt (1 Tbsp), brown sugar (4 Tbsp), oyster sauce (2 Tbsp), soy sauce (2 Tbsp), hoisin sauce (2 Tbsp), red wine vinegar (2 Tbsp), and mirin (1 Tbsp).
Add pork to the marinade and refrigerate for 8-24 hours.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Place pork on a wire rack set over a pan with about ¼ cup water (add more water as needed to prevent burning).
Reserve extra marinade. Roast the pork and baste with the marinade every 15 minutes.
After 45 minutes (on the third baste), switch to a diluted honey glaze and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove from oven and let rest for 10 minutes.
Slice and serve over rice with steamed vegetables and sauce of your choice.
Cook the egg noodles (8 oz) according to package directions. Drain and set aside.

Whisk together the soy sauce (1/4 cup), water (1/4 cup), rice wine vinegar (3 Tbsp), sesame oil (2 Tbsp), honey (2 Tbsp), Sriracha (2 Tbsp), oyster sauce (2 Tbsp), chili sauce (1 Tbsp), garlic (4 cloves, minced), ground ginger (1 tsp), black pepper (1 tsp), and salt (1 tsp). Set aside.
Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef (1 lb) and cook, breaking it up, until browned, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste. Drain fat. Remove beef and set aside.

Return the pan to medium-high heat. Add the onion (1 medium, sliced) and carrots (3, sliced into strips). Stir fry 3-4 minutes until beginning to soften. Add the frozen broccoli (1 bag, 12 oz) and stir fry another 2-3 minutes.
Return the beef (1 lb, cooked) to the pan. Add the noodles (8 oz, cooked) and pour in the sauce. Toss to combine and cook 2 minutes.
Stir together the cornstarch (1 Tbsp) and water (2 Tbsp) to form a slurry. Add to the pan and stir until the sauce tightens, about 1 minute.
Remove from heat. Add the fresh spinach (3 cups) and let sit 2 minutes off heat to steam and wilt. Toss to incorporate and serve.

ingredients.text: 2.2 lbs rindless pork belly, sliced and halved (each piece about finger-length)
1 thumb-sized piece fresh ginger, peeled and minced
4 thumb-sized pieces fresh ginger, peeled and minced
1 tsp rice vinegar (added to reserved portion only)
2 medium carrots, sliced on the bias

instructions.detail: Add pork belly (2.2 lbs), hot chicken stock (4 1/4 cups), minced ginger (1 thumb-sized piece), garlic (3 cloves, roughly chopped), rice wine (1 Tbsp), and sugar (1 Tbsp) to a heavy-based pot or cast iron casserole. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 2 hours.
Remove pork from the braising liquid and pat thoroughly dry with paper towels. Reserve the liquid if desired — excellent base for an Asian noodle soup. Cut pork into bite-sized chunks.

Whisk together minced ginger (4 thumb-sized pieces), red chilies (4, finely chopped), honey (1/2 cup), brown sugar (1/2 cup), dark soy sauce (3/4 cup), garlic powder (1 Tbsp), and onion powder (1 Tbsp) in a bowl. Divide into 4 equal portions. Transfer 1 portion to a small bowl, stir in rice vinegar (1 tsp), and set aside. The remaining 3 portions go into the pan.

Heat half the vegetable oil (1/4 cup) in a wok or large skillet over medium-high. Add onion (1 medium, sliced) and carrots (2 medium, sliced on the bias); cook stirring until softened, about 4 minutes. Add broccoli florets (1 head) and cook until bright green and just tender-crisp, about 3-4 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer to a large bowl and set aside.

Heat remaining vegetable oil (1/4 cup) in the same pan over medium-high. Add pork chunks (2.2 lbs) with a pinch of salt and pepper. Fry, turning regularly, until golden and beginning to crisp on the edges, about 5-7 minutes. Use a splatter guard — rendered pork fat will pop aggressively at this stage.

Add diced red bell pepper (1) and cook until softened to your preference, about 2-3 minutes. Pour 3/4 of the total glaze (3 portions) over the pork and pepper. Cook, turning the pork frequently, until dark and sticky, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked vegetables back in and fold into the pork mixture until evenly coated. For large batches, add in two additions so the pan stays hot.

Pour the reserved glaze portion with rice vinegar (1 tsp) over everything and fold to combine. If the sauce needs thickening, sprinkle in xanthan gum (start with 1/4 tsp) and toss until the sauce tightens to your liking.
Serve over cooked white rice topped with chopped spring onions and chopped red chilies.
Soak basmati rice (1½ cups) in a big bowl of water for at least 30 minutes.
Coat chicken with salt and the full spice blend. Reserve extra spice blend.
Add oil (3 Tbsp) to a pan and heat up. Add garlic (4 cloves) and onion (1 red). Season with salt, pepper, and half the reserved spice mix. Sauté well, then set aside.
In the same pan, add marinated chicken and cook for about 5 minutes each side. Set chicken aside.
Add water (2 cups) to pan and bring to a boil. Add soaked rice. Add turmeric (¼ tsp) and remaining spice mix.
Simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes until rice is cooked.
Add chicken back on top of rice. Garnish with fried onions, garlic, and almonds.

ingredients.text: ¼ cup spring onions, finely chopped
1 Tbsp dried mint, finely chopped (fresh is also good)
¼ cup extra virgin olive oil

instructions.detail: Place washed potatoes in a large pot, cover with cold water by about 2 inches. Bring to a gentle boil over high heat, then reduce to medium. Cook until fork-tender - do not overcook.
Let potatoes cool slightly. Cut into even bite-sized chunks (with or without skin).
In a large bowl, add potato chunks, spring onions (¼ cup), parsley (¼ cup), mint (1 Tbsp), and optional garlic.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil (¼ cup), lemon juice (¼ cup), salt, and black pepper.
Pour dressing over potato mixture and gently toss to combine. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve warm or chilled.
Blend onion, parsley, and mint in a food processor until smooth.
Crush garlic into a paste.
Add all spices. Mix with ground beef (2 lb). Let sit for 1-24 hours.
Form meat onto skewers, no more than 1 inch in diameter.
Cook on grill over medium heat until internal temp reaches 145°F.
Add all ingredients to a mixing bowl and combine.
Adjust salt if needed. Serve cold.

ingredients.text: Scant ¾ cup mayonnaise (or vegan mayo)
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste

instructions.detail: Whisk together mayo, apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, maple syrup, celery seeds, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper until smooth.
Pour dressing over 6–8 cups coleslaw mix (1 standard store bag). Toss to coat evenly.
Refrigerate for 1–4 hours, stirring occasionally, to let flavors develop.
Keep refrigerated until ready to serve.

ingredients.text: ½ cup (50g) oat flour or almond flour
⅓ cup (80g) peanut butter or almond butter
¼ tsp salt (skip if using salted peanut butter)

instructions.detail: Combine all ingredients in a bowl until a dough forms.
Roll into 1-inch balls.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes before serving.

ingredients.text: 2 Tbsp brown rice syrup or honey
Chocolate chips, nuts, or dried fruit (optional)

instructions.detail: Combine all ingredients in a bowl until a dough forms.
Roll into balls and refrigerate until firm.

ingredients.text: ¼ cup shredded coconut (optional - note: makes them dry)

instructions.detail: Combine all ingredients. If mixture is too dry, add a splash of water.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes. Roll into 1-inch balls. Store at room temperature (shelf stable).

ingredients.text: 1 cup vanilla or unflavored protein powder
2 Tbsp nut butter (almond, peanut, or cashew)
Dark chocolate chips or cacao nibs

instructions.detail: Blend cottage cheese (1 cup) until smooth. Combine with all other ingredients.
Fold in any optional mix-ins.
Roll into balls and refrigerate until firm.

ingredients.text: A splash of water if needed

instructions.detail: Combine all ingredients. Roll into 1-inch balls, adding a splash of water if mixture is too dry.
Refrigerate until firm.

ingredients.text: ¼ cup raw honey (or maple syrup for vegan)

instructions.detail: Combine all ingredients. Roll into 1-inch balls.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.

ingredients.text: 3 Tbsp pure maple syrup or honey

instructions.detail: Combine all ingredients. Chill dough for 15 minutes. Roll into 1-inch balls. Refrigerate until firm.

ingredients.text: ½ cup protein powder (chocolate or vanilla)
⅓ cup coconut sugar or granulated sweetener of choice

instructions.detail: Preheat oven to 350°F and line a pan with parchment paper.
Add all ingredients to a bowl and mix well.
Transfer to baking pan and spread evenly. Optionally top with chocolate chips. Bake for 20-25 minutes until firm and slightly risen.
Let cool completely. Top with flaky sea salt and enjoy.

ingredients.text: ½ cup Greek yogurt (plain or vanilla)
2 Tbsp peanut butter or almond butter
¼ cup oat flour or almond flour

instructions.detail: Mix Greek yogurt (½ cup), peanut butter (2 Tbsp), honey (1 Tbsp), and vanilla (½ tsp) in a bowl until well combined.
Add oat flour (¼ cup) and stir until a dough forms.
Gently fold in chocolate chips (1 Tbsp).
Divide dough into two equal portions. Roll into balls and flatten slightly into a cookie shape.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up.
Enjoy chilled. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days.
Simmer whole chicken in broth (6 cups) and water (2 cups) until cooked through. Remove chicken, cool, and shred meat.
Return broth to a boil. Add carrots (3 cups), celery (2 cups), onion (2 cups), and garlic (3 cloves). Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add egg noodles (2 cups), shredded chicken, BBQ sauce (1 Tbsp), and dill weed (2 tsp). Simmer until noodles are tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot.
Simmer chicken in broth (6 cups) and water (2 cups). Remove, cool, and shred.
Add onion (1), celery (3 stalks), garlic (3 cloves), and sun-dried tomatoes (½ cup) to broth. Simmer 10 minutes.
Add pasta shells (8 oz) and cook until al dente.
Stir in heavy cream (1 cup), Parmesan (1 cup), and white wine vinegar (½ cup). Add shredded chicken back.
Mix cornstarch (2 tsp) with water (2 tsp). Stir into soup to thicken. Add spinach (2 cups) and cook 2 more minutes.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot.
Blend all ingredients together. Coat chicken and marinate for at least 1-2 hours before cooking.
Whisk all ingredients together. Coat chicken and marinate for at least 1 hour before cooking.
Whisk all ingredients together. Coat chicken and marinate for at least 1 hour before cooking.
Combine all ingredients. Toss with sliced chicken and marinate for 15-30 minutes before stir-frying (velveting technique).
Marinate chicken (1 lb) with yogurt (½ cup), lemon juice (1 Tbsp), ginger-garlic paste (1 Tbsp), chili powder (1 tsp), garam masala (1 tsp), salt (1 tsp). Rest 30 min+.
Sauté chopped onion (1 large) in butter (2 Tbsp). Add garlic (2 cloves, minced) and ginger (1 inch, grated).
Add tomato puree (1 cup), chili powder (1 tsp), and coriander (1 tsp). Simmer 10 minutes.
Add marinated chicken and cook until browned and cooked through.
Stir in heavy cream (½ cup) and butter (2 Tbsp). Finish with crushed fenugreek leaves (1 tsp).
Heat oil (2 Tbsp) and sauté cumin seeds (1 tsp), onion (1 large, chopped), and garlic (3 cloves, minced).
Add tomatoes (2, diced), chili powder (1 tsp), turmeric (½ tsp), and coriander (2 tsp). Cook until soft.
Add chickpeas (2 cups, drained) and water (½ cup). Simmer 20 minutes.
Add garam masala (1 tsp) and chopped cilantro (¼ cup). Serve with rice or naan.
Brown lamb cubes (1.5 lb) in oil (3 Tbsp). Remove and set aside.
Add onion (2, sliced), garlic (4 cloves), and ginger (1 Tbsp). Fry until golden.
Stir in paprika (2 tsp), cumin (1 tsp), coriander (1 tsp), turmeric (½ tsp), and chili powder (½ tsp).
Add lamb back with yogurt (1 cup). Stir well.
Pour in water (1 cup). Simmer 1.5 hours until tender.

ingredients.text: 3 cups mixed vegetables (zucchini, bell pepper, eggplant)

instructions.detail: Fry green curry paste (2 Tbsp) in oil (1 Tbsp) for 1 minute.
Add mixed vegetables (3 cups, chopped). Cook 5 minutes.
Stir in vegetable stock (2 cups), soy sauce (2 Tbsp), and brown sugar (1 tsp).
Simmer 10 minutes. Add fresh basil (½ cup) and lime juice (1 Tbsp). Serve with rice.
Brown beef chunks (1.5 lb) in oil (2 Tbsp). Remove.
Fry onion (2, chopped), garlic (4 cloves), and ginger (1 Tbsp).
Stir in chili powder (2 tsp), turmeric (1 tsp), cumin (1 tsp), and coriander (2 tsp) for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes (1 cup) and beef back. Add water (1.5 cups). Simmer 1.5 hours.
Stir in vinegar (1 Tbsp) before serving.
Sauté onion (1, sliced), carrot (2, diced), and potato (2, cubed) in oil (2 Tbsp).
Add chicken (1 lb, in chunks) and cook until browned.
Pour in chicken stock (4 cups). Simmer 20 minutes.
Stir in Japanese curry roux blocks (4 pieces) until thick and saucy.
Serve over steamed rice.
Sauté paneer cubes (12 oz) in oil (2 Tbsp) until golden. Remove and set aside.
Fry onion (1), garlic (2 cloves), ginger (1 tsp), and cumin (1 tsp) in the same pan.
Add spinach (10 oz), chili powder (1 tsp), turmeric (½ tsp), and salt (1 tsp). Cook until soft.
Blend the spinach mixture, then return to pan. Add paneer back.
Stir in cream (¼ cup) and heat through.
Heat oil (2 Tbsp). Fry onion (1), garlic (3 cloves), ginger (1 tsp).
Add chili powder (1 tsp), turmeric (½ tsp), coriander (1 tsp), and cumin (1 tsp).
Add chopped tomatoes (2 cups). Simmer 10 minutes.
Add white fish pieces (1 lb). Simmer 10 minutes until cooked through.
Stir in tamarind paste (1 Tbsp) and cilantro (2 Tbsp, chopped).
Rinse red lentils (1 cup) well.
Fry mustard seeds (1 tsp) and cumin seeds (1 tsp) in oil (2 Tbsp) until they pop.
Add onion (1), garlic (3 cloves), curry leaves (6), and green chili (1).
Stir in turmeric (½ tsp) and chili powder (1 tsp).
Add lentils and water (3 cups). Simmer 20 minutes until creamy.

name: South African Bunny Chow (Chicken Curry in Bread)

instructions.detail: Fry onion (1), garlic (3 cloves), ginger (1 tsp) in oil (2 Tbsp).
Add chicken (1 lb, cubed) and brown well.
Stir in curry powder (2 Tbsp), cumin (1 tsp), coriander (1 tsp), turmeric (½ tsp).
Add tomato puree (1 cup) and water (1 cup). Simmer 30 minutes.
Hollow out a large bread loaf and fill with curry.

ingredients.text: 1 lb chicken breast, cooked and shredded

instructions.detail: Heat olive oil (2 Tbsp) in a large pot. Add onion (1), carrots (2), celery (2 stalks), and garlic (3 cloves). Sauté until softened.
Pour in chicken broth (8 cups) and bring to a boil.
Stir in shredded chicken (1 lb) and egg noodles (3 cups).
Add salt (1 tsp) and black pepper (½ tsp). Simmer until noodles are tender. Garnish with parsley (2 Tbsp).
Melt butter (2 Tbsp) in a soup pot. Sauté onion (1), celery (2 stalks), and carrots (3) until softened.
Add diced chicken thighs (1 lb), thyme (1 tsp), and bay leaf (1).
Pour in chicken stock (10 cups). Bring to a boil, then reduce to simmer.
Add egg noodles (4 cups) and cook until tender.
Season with salt (1 tsp) and black pepper (½ tsp). Remove bay leaf before serving.
Melt butter (4 Tbsp) in a large pot. Add carrots (2), celery (2 stalks), and onion (1). Cook until softened.
Stir in flour (¼ cup) and cook for 2 minutes.
Slowly whisk in chicken broth (6 cups). Bring to a boil. Add wild rice (1 cup) and simmer until tender.
Stir in shredded chicken (2 cups) and heavy cream (1 cup). Season with salt (1 tsp) and pepper (½ tsp).

ingredients.text: 1 can (10 oz) diced tomatoes with green chilies
1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed

instructions.detail: Heat olive oil (2 Tbsp) in a large pot. Add onion (1), garlic (3 cloves), and red bell pepper (1). Sauté until softened.
Add chicken broth (6 cups), diced tomatoes (1 can), black beans (1 can), and corn (1 cup).
Stir in shredded chicken (3 cups), chili powder (1 tsp), and cumin (1 tsp). Simmer 15 minutes.
Stir in cheddar cheese (2 cups) until melted.
Top with crushed tortilla chips (2 cups). Season with salt (1 tsp) and pepper (½ tsp).
Heat olive oil (2 Tbsp). Add onion (1), garlic (3 cloves), carrots (2), and celery (2 stalks). Cook until softened.
Add diced chicken thighs (1 lb) and cook until lightly browned.

instructions.step: Version 1 - The Woks of Life (Char Siu)

Whisk light soy sauce (2 tbsp), hoisin sauce (2 tbsp), Shaoxing wine (1 tbsp), honey (2 tbsp), sugar (1 tbsp), five-spice (1 tsp), white pepper (1/4 tsp), sesame oil (1/2 tsp), and garlic (from 3 cloves) until smooth.

Roast at 425°F (218°C) on a rack 20 minutes, brush with honey (1 tbsp) mixed with hot water (1 tbsp), flip and roast 15-20 minutes more until 145°F (63°C).
Rest 5 minutes, then slice. Brush with reserved glaze (2 tbsp) if desired.
